title: Language Coverage and Vocab Size
concept: Multilingual models require larger vocabularies (e.g. 250,000 in XLM-RoBERTa) to represent characters and subwords across 100+ languages without overlap.

exercises.prompt: What is the main superpower of a "multilingual" AI model?

options.text: They can understand and process multiple languages.
They only speak one language but very fast.
They translate everything into English before reading it.

exercises.explain: Multilingual AI models are trained on text from many different languages, allowing them to translate, analyze, and generate text globally.

cards.title: Speaking the World's Tongues
cards.body: If you speak English, Spanish, and Mandarin, your brain holds words and symbols from three different systems. AI models face the same challenge! When an AI tries to learn 100+ languages at once, it needs a mind-bogglingly huge vocabulary. Let's find out why!

cards.title: Language Coverage & Vocab Size
cards.body: Language coverage refers to how many different languages an AI model can understand. To support dozens of languages, a model needs a massive vocabulary size—meaning a giant library of saved words, characters, and subword pieces (like 250,000 tokens in XLM-RoBERTa) so it doesn't get confused between different alphabets.

cards.title: The Giant Suitcase
cards.body: Imagine a small suitcase that only holds clothes for a summer beach trip. That is a single-language model. Now, imagine a giant trunk packed with outfits for snowy mountains, deserts, and rainy cities all at once! That giant trunk is a multilingual vocabulary—it needs to be massive to hold the unique gear (words and characters) for every destination.

cards.title: XLM-RoBERTa's Super-Library
cards.body: A model trained only on English might need a vocabulary of only 30,000 words. But a multilingual model like XLM-RoBERTa supports over 100 languages, including English, Hindi, Greek, and Chinese. Because it has to store Greek letters, Chinese characters, and Hindi script, its vocabulary size swells to 250,000 tokens!

cards.title: Does Bigger Mean Slower?
cards.body: You might think that because a model has a huge vocabulary, it must be slow or bad at English. Actually, having a large vocabulary allows the model to handle many languages simultaneously with great accuracy, without losing its grasp on English!

cards.title: Vocabulary Scale Comparison
cards.body: Compare the vocabulary needs of a single-language model versus a global multilingual model.

right.text: Huge vocabulary (~250k tokens). Needs space for Greek, Arabic, Chinese, and Hindi scripts.

left.text: Small vocabulary (~30k tokens). Only needs English characters and words.

exercises.prompt: Approximately how large is the vocabulary of the multilingual model XLM-RoBERTa?
exercises.explain: XLM-RoBERTa has a vocabulary of around 250,000 tokens to cover over 100 languages successfully.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A model trained only on English needs a larger vocabulary than a model trained on 100 different languages.
exercises.explain: Multilingual models require much larger vocabularies because they must store unique characters and subwords for 100+ different languages.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of multilingual AI models.
exercises.sentence: A multilingual model needs a much larger vocabulary to store characters from many languages.
exercises.explain: To support dozens of languages, a multilingual AI model must expand its vocabulary size to store all the different letters and symbols.

exercises.prompt: Two of these statements about multilingual vocabularies are correct facts, but one is a false AI hallucination. Spot the AI hallucination!

options.text: Multilingual models use subword tokenization to share common roots across similar languages.
AI models divide their 250,000 vocabulary slots equally, giving exactly 2,500 words to each of the 100 languages.
A large vocabulary prevents characters from different alphabets (like Cyrillic and Latin) from overlapping.

exercises.explain: The second option is generated by an AI because it hallucinates a fake rule. Multilingual vocabularies do not strictly divide space equally; they dynamically store tokens based on overall language data.

exercises.prompt: Why do multilingual AI models need giant vocabularies of 250,000+ words/symbols?

options.text: It keeps the model's file size smaller.
It allows the model to translate words instantly without neural network processing.
It lets the model represent characters and subwords for 100+ languages without overlap.
It forces the model to only use English characters.

exercises.explain: Multilingual models like XLM-RoBERTa need massive vocabularies (often 250,000 tokens or more) to represent the alphabets, symbols, and subwords of over 100 different languages without running out of space or messy overlap.
